site.name: DevRelJam
site.description: DevRelJam brings experienced Developer Relations practitioners together for practical, candid meetups around advocacy, community, engagement, and the technologies we represent.

seo.defaultDescription: Peer-led DevRel meetups for practitioners to exchange wins, missteps, and field-tested ideas around advocacy, community, and engagement.
seo.author: Yashraj Nayak

hero.headline: DevRelJam
hero.body: A practitioner-first community initiative where DevRel folks jam on what worked, what did not, and what we are learning while building developer communities.

format.title: How Jams Work
format.description: Every Jam is built around a small, useful room: clear agenda, peer-led sessions, candid discussion, and enough community time for the best conversations to continue.

split.label: Practitioner sessions
split.body: Field-tested talks from DevRel, advocacy, community, and adjacent technology leaders.

split.label: AMA and discussion
split.body: Candid room-led exchange on programs, metrics, developer audiences, tooling, and mistakes.

split.label: Community time
split.body: Intentional networking before, between, and after sessions so the best ideas do not stay on stage.

principles.title: Curated room
principles.body: Registration is free but approval-based so the room stays useful for working practitioners.

principles.title: Conference-adjacent
principles.body: Jams are planned when DevRel teams are already near major developer gatherings.

principles.title: Real notes over polish
principles.body: The format rewards lessons learned, honest tradeoffs, and operational details.

about.title: What is DevRelJam?
paragraphs: DevRelJam is a first-of-its-kind meetup series for Developer Relations professionals and adjacent community builders. It is not a generic tech meetup; the room is built for people who have shipped programs, worked with developer audiences, and want sharper peer conversations.

Each Jam is intentionally practical: small enough for honest exchange, structured enough to leave with useful ideas, and timed around major tech gatherings so participation is easier for travelling DevRel teams.

highlights.title: Peer-led format
highlights.body: Sessions prioritize field notes, lessons learned, and candid discussion over polished vendor talks.

highlights.title: Experienced audience
highlights.body: Built for DevRel practitioners, community leads, advocates, program managers, and people in adjacent roles.

highlights.title: Conference-adjacent
highlights.body: Jams happen when the right people are already in town, making high-quality hallway conversations easier.

events.title: Upcoming DevRelJam
events.description: The next Jam is live on Luma. Registration is free and mandatory, with host approval required.

current.name: DevRelJam Singapore - September 2026
current.description: Join DevRel practitioners for a focused evening of peer-led sessions, an Ask Me Anything block, and practical conversations about developer advocacy, community, and engagement.

past.title: Past Jams
past.description: Browse previous DevRelJam editions, agendas, and speaker themes from cities where the community has already gathered.

items.name: DevRelJam Bengaluru - April 2025
items.summary: The first DevRelJam gathered experienced practitioners alongside GIDS 2025 for talks, an AMA, and room-led discussion.
highlights: The Devil Is In The Titles by Ram Iyengar
AMA with James Ward, Ivar Grimstad, and Sourabh Bagrecha
The Engaged Developer Funnel by Stephen Chin

items.name: DevRel Jam Hyderabad - August 2025
items.summary: A KubeCon India 2025-adjacent Jam designed around duo lightning talks, networking, and a practitioner panel.

items.name: DevRelJam Bengaluru - September 2025
items.summary: A Bengaluru edition supported by Arbitrum India with business alignment, developer relations foundations, and a practitioner AMA.
highlights: Aligning DevRel with the Business by Ben Greenberg
AMA with Anuj Duggal, Ben Greenberg, Juhi Singh, Yashraj Nayak, and Shivay Lamba
Introducing the Developer Relations Foundation by Aditya Oberai

items.name: DevRelJam Singapore - February 2026
items.summary: A Singapore edition with social impact, DevRel identity, and GenAI-era developer relations on the agenda.
highlights: Social Impact IS Your DevRel Strategy by Annie Mathew
Hey DevRel, Who Do You Work For? by Donnie Prakoso
DevRel in the Era of GenAI by Thu Ya Kyaw

items.name: DevRelJam Bengaluru - April 2026
items.summary: A GIDS 2026-adjacent edition with practitioner talks, an AMA block, and dinner-time community exchange.
highlights: Business Skills DevRel Pros Should Learn by Atsushi Nakatsugawa
Helping Developers Discover the Right AI Tools by Nimit Savant
AMA with James Ward, Mary Grygleski, Brian Benz, and Yashraj Nayak

cities.title: Where We Jam
cities.description: DevRelJam is already mapped across cities where developer communities, conferences, and DevRel teams naturally gather.

speakers.title: Speak at a Future Jam
speakers.description: Have a field-tested lesson, community experiment, program failure, or technical advocacy pattern that other DevRel practitioners should hear? Speaker submissions are open on Sessionize.

footer.note: Thoughtful DevRel practitioners, useful conversations, fewer buzzwords.
footer.builtWith: Built with vanilla JavaScript and hosted on GitHub Pages.
